summary: Promoted ADR 0299 into canonical mainline truth as repository version `0.177.152` and platform version `0.130.95` after replaying ntfy from the latest realistic `origin/main` baseline at repository version `0.177.151` and platform version `0.130.94`, verifying the governed public health and publish paths, preserving the intentional immutable-guest guardrail and the first exact-main restic-hook failure as evidence, fixing the live-apply-trigger restic credential contract, and rerunning the exact-main `live-apply-service` wrapper to a full green result.

verification.check: Protected-surface cut to canonical mainline truth
verification.observed: The first exact-main `release_manager.py --bump patch` run wrote the protected repository surfaces for `0.177.152` and `0.130.95` successfully before the external Outline sync failed with `502`, preserved in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-04-03-adr-0299-mainline-release-manager-r1-0.177.152.txt`; the follow-up rerun with `LV3_SKIP_OUTLINE_SYNC=1` confirmed there were no remaining `Unreleased` bullets to consume, preserved in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-04-03-adr-0299-mainline-release-manager-r2-0.177.152.txt`.

verification.check: Latest-main ntfy converge and governed publish path
verification.observed: The rebased latest-main worktree passed `make converge-ntfy env=production`, the live `/opt/ntfy/server.yml` auth contract matched the governed publisher and ACL expectations, `https://ntfy.lv3.org/v1/health` returned successfully, and the direct controller publish to `https://ntfy.lv3.org/platform-ansible-info` plus the normalized `scripts/ntfy_publish.py` helper proof are preserved in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-04-03-ws-0299-branch-verification.txt`.

verification.check: Exact-main immutable-guest gate and ntfy service replay
verification.observed: The first exact-main `make live-apply-service service=ntfy env=production` run correctly stopped at the immutable-guest replacement policy for the `edge_and_stateful` ntfy service, preserved in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-04-03-adr-0299-mainline-live-apply-r1-0.177.152.txt`. The final rerun with `ALLOW_IN_PLACE_MUTATION=true` completed successfully with recap `docker-runtime-lv3 : ok=110 changed=2 failed=0`, `localhost : ok=19 changed=0 failed=0`, and `nginx-lv3 : ok=49 changed=5 failed=0`, preserved in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-04-03-adr-0299-mainline-live-apply-r3-0.177.152.txt`.

The first in-place exact-main replay reached the shared restic post-hook and failed because the runtime credential payload was missing `ntfy_token`, preserved in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-04-03-adr-0299-mainline-live-apply-r2-0.177.152.txt`. This workstream narrowed `scripts/restic_config_backup.py` so `ntfy_token` remains required for scheduled backup notification flows but no longer blocks `--live-apply-trigger` runs that never send ntfy notifications; the focused regression tests live in `tests/test_restic_config_backup.py`, and the final exact-main replay synced `receipts/restic-backups/20260403T080214Z.json` plus `receipts/restic-snapshots-latest.json` with `status: ok`.

verification.check: Exact-main repository automation and validation bundle
verification.observed: The exact-main tree passed the focused pytest replay with `195 passed`, repository data-model validation, live-apply receipt validation, generated-doc validation, workstream-surface and agent-standard validation, `git diff --check`, and `make pre-push-gate`. The pre-push gate preserved the shared remote-builder Atlas timeout in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-04-03-adr-0299-mainline-pre-push-gate-r3.txt`, then reran the unresolved `atlas-lint` check locally and finished green; the direct local Atlas lint replay is preserved in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-04-03-adr-0299-mainline-atlas-lint-r1.txt`.

notes: This exact-main receipt is the canonical proof for ADR 0299 on repository version `0.177.152` and platform version `0.130.95`.
The evidence intentionally preserves the first blocked exact-main wrapper run and the first restic-hook failure because both exposed real platform controls instead of operator mistakes.
The first release-manager run still preserves the external Outline `502`, but the protected repository surfaces were already written correctly before that external dependency failed.

The pre-push gate also preserves a shared remote-builder Atlas timeout; the gate still exited green because the intended local fallback reran the unresolved `atlas-lint` check successfully on this exact-main tree.

correction_loop.surface: exact-main ntfy live-apply wrapper post-run restic backup trigger
correction_loop.repair_action: Kept the immutable-guest guardrail intact, captured the first restic-hook failure, narrowed `scripts/restic_config_backup.py` so `ntfy_token` remains mandatory for scheduled notification flows but does not block `--live-apply-trigger` backups, added focused regression coverage, and reran the exact-main wrapper to a green result.
